item.title: Testing your API with `pytest-mock`
item.description: For years I’ve told myself I want to take part in Fantasy Premier League (FPL), and it seems like as good an excuse as any to flex some data science + engineering chops. One of the obvious data sources to start looking at is FPL’s official API, which I learned about from David Allen’s great write up. The endpoint given there seems to dump its entire dataset in one big json response.

item.title: How I set up continuous deployment for my blog via Github Actions
item.description: I thought I’d christen this blog in the traditional way, by talking about how I set it up in the first place. I’m using Hugo to build this site, and at some point I’ll go through the set up I used, but this post is specifically about how I use Github Actions to deploy this blog from a (free, private) source repo to a (public) target repo which uses Github Pages.

item.title: About
item.description: My name is Steve, I’m a data scientist living in Berlin.
